triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income Ratio
triageFlags.description: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to the unsustainable Debt-to-Income ratio.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum DTI for Tier C applicants is 35%. The applicant's DTI is calculated at 11000%, which is significantly above the acceptable limit, indicating a high risk of financial overextension.

triageFlags.title: Sub-Prime Credit Score
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score places them in the sub-prime category, requiring stricter lending criteria.
triageFlags.recommendation: Proceed with caution and consider additional security or a higher deposit if other conditions are met.
triageFlags.reasoning: The applicant's credit score of 575 categorizes them as Tier C (Sub-Prime), which requires stricter lending criteria, including a minimum deposit of 10% of the vehicle's Goods Cost.

content.reasoning: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier, and their credit score places them in a high-risk category. These factors combined indicate a high risk of default.
